rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN M. POTASZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues have not cleared: (1) Petition alleges that the estate has a 25% interest in the real property; however, I&A was filed setting forth 50%. (2) It appears the estate should be in the condition to close as Executor will propose the distribution of the asset (real property) without a sale as stated on page 6. (3) Notice of Hearing not filed. (4) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance not filed. (5) Petition needs to include prayer. (6) Proposed order to be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

If neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate is filed before 8/28/24, an appearance will be required on 8/28/24. rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KEVIN KA-CHEUNG YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/28/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (2) The Wells Fargo Advisers statement for the period 9/1/22 - 9/30/22 is not the statement immediately preceding the date the conservator was appointed; the assets shown in the I&A cannot be verified by that statement. (3) The attorney's fee declaration does not appear to have been filed yet. (4) There is no schedule of liabilities in the accounting. None appears to have been attached to the supplement filed on 7/22/24. Deadline is 8/16/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. (A supplement or amendment to a pleading should stand on its own and not "answer" issues raised by an examiner in tentative rulings.) No courtesy copies have been received yet by the examiner as of 7/24/24. If Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES W. MOSSER 2007 TRUST DATED JUNE 10, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: 1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; 2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser: 3) Recovery Of Property; And 4) Order Compelling Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Trust petitions require 30 days' notice, and Probate Code Section 851 requires 30 days' notice with a copy of the petition. (2) The "supplement" e-filed on 7/16/24 actually amended the petition, so the caption was misleading. (3) An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (4) It is not sufficient for the attorney for a party entitled to notice to agree to accept electronic service of notice on behalf of the party; the party must direct in writing that notice be given to the attorney only. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b) and Probate Code Section 1214.] (5) In any event, there does not appear to be anything in the records showing that the attorney for the respondent agreed to electronic service of notice. (6) A courtesy copy of the petition and related papers still have not been provided. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A petition should be amended or supplemented by the petitioner, as appropriate, or missing papers should simply be delivered to the court; there is no need to "respond" to issues, whether they are in formal notes or posted in a tentative ruling. The petitioner is merely informed and believes that other beneficiaries' interests have been satisfied, but she does not provide any support for this.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETTIE BERNETT HIGHTOWER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/16/24. The attorney's "second supplemental declaration" e-filed on 7/15/24 was unnecessary and unhelpful. A proposed fiduciary must sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto, and there is no need for an attorney to narrate about papers that will be filed; the missing papers should simply be filed. In any event, no amended petition was filed concurrently with the attorney's second supplemental declaration, as the attorney swore to in his declaration. Deadline is 9/6/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (This Court has not required the local form Declaration of Real Property since November 2020.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETTIE BERNETT HIGHTOWER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/16/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Items 2d and 3e of the petition are silent regarding bond. If not all heirs waive bond, and the petitioner seeks full IAEA authority, bond will be for the full estimated value of the estate. (3) Item 5b of the petition must be answered. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) No proof of publication has been filed. Deadline is 9/6/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAREN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/19/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Item 3e of the petition is incomplete. (3) Item 3g(2) of the petition does not indicate how the petitioners have priority. (4) Items 5a(3) or (4) and (7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (5) Item 7 of the petition must be completed if the decedent was not survived by a spouse or issue. (6) Item 8 of the petition does not indicate the RELATIONSHIP to the decedent of the persons listed. If they are "next of kin," item 8 must clearly identify the decedent's parents, grandparents, siblings, and cousins, if any, who predeceased her and show their dates of death. [LRSF 14.18 C.] (7) The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties & Liabilities must be filed. (8) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/9/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If the two petitioners are not the only parties entitled to notice, notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.])
